title: Specialized Trackers — Inheritance
description: Create a class hierarchy using inheritance. Define a base Tracker class with virtual methods for calculating averages and displaying students. Then create a derived WeightedGradeBook class that overrides calculateAverage to apply weighted grades (e.g., homework 30%, midterm 30%, final 40%). Demonstrate polymorphism by using a base class pointer.
test_code:
assert 'class' in cpp, 'Must define classes'
assert ':' in cpp.split('class')[2] if cpp.count('class') >= 2 else False, 'Must use inheritance (: public BaseClass)'
assert 'virtual' in cpp, 'Must use virtual keyword for polymorphic methods'
assert 'override' in cpp or 'Override' in cpp, 'Must use override keyword on derived class methods'
assert 'Tracker' in cpp, 'Must define a Tracker base class'
assert 'WeightedGradeBook' in cpp or 'Weighted' in cpp, 'Must define a WeightedGradeBook derived class'
assert 'protected' in cpp or 'public' in cpp, 'Must use access specifiers in class hierarchy'
assert 'weight' in cpp.lower(), 'WeightedGradeBook must use weights for grade calculation'
acceptance_criteria: Defines a Tracker base class with virtual methods
Defines a WeightedGradeBook class that inherits from Tracker
WeightedGradeBook overrides calculateStudentAverage with weighted calculation
Uses virtual keyword on base class methods
Uses override keyword on derived class methods
Demonstrates polymorphism (base class pointer or reference)
hints: Base class: class Tracker { protected: vector<Student> roster; public: virtual double calculateStudentAverage(double grades[], int size) { ... } };
Derived class: class WeightedGradeBook : public Tracker { ... };
Override: double calculateStudentAverage(double grades[], int size) override { return grades[0]*0.3 + grades[1]*0.3 + grades[2]*0.4; }
Polymorphism: Tracker* tracker = new WeightedGradeBook(); tracker->displayAll();
Always define a virtual destructor in the base class: virtual ~Tracker() {}
